title: Auto close panel after close last tab not work, and not exist options in "Options"
author: Apollon-Theos
body_snippet:
After I close last tab in panel, panel continue exist. Empty.

## Expected Behavior
Empty panel must be closed automatically, without think about this and mouse click.

## Current Behavior
Panel need close manually.

## Possible Solution
Add option about this in "Options", if this "by design" :)

## Steps to Reproduce (for bugs)
1. Close last tab and look.
2.
3.
4.

## Context
<!--- How has this issue affected you? What are you trying to accomplish? -->
<!--- Providing context...

iterations.description: The issue describes a missing option for automatically closing panels after the last tab is closed, which is a desirable feature or a bug in expected behavior.

comments.author: kees-z
comments.snippet: I can confirm this. Version 1.76.20 automatically closes empty panels. V1.77 Prerelease and Nightly build don't close the panel after closing the last tab. Additionally, launching an external app created a new panel that doesn't close if the external app is not integrated. Version 1.76 doesn't leave an empty panel open in this case.

comments.author: farosch
comments.snippet:
@Apollon-Theos @kees-z 
Do you have the option active to "always show connection tabs"?

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The issue describes a missing option for automatically closing panels after the last tab is closed, which is a desirable feature or a bug in expected behavior.
Approach: Add a new setting in FrmOptions to allow users to configure auto-closing of panels when the last tab is closed, and implement the corresponding logic in FrmMain to manage panel visibility based on this setting.
